content:
Using LLMs to build personal knowledge bases is a powerful approach for organizing and exploring research topics. The system centers on automated ingestion, indexing, and synthesis of diverse sources into a structured wiki. This wiki, stored as markdown files with images, is continuously maintained and queried by the LLM agent.

• Source materials like articles, papers, and images are stored in a raw directory and compiled by an LLM into a structured, linked markdown wiki with summaries and backlinks.  
• Obsidian serves as the visual interface to navigate raw data, wiki pages, and visual outputs, while the LLM handles nearly all content creation and updates.  
• Once sufficiently developed, the wiki enables complex Q&A, where the LLM researches and synthesizes answers, often generating new markdown, slides, or visualizations.  
• Additional tools like search interfaces and LLM-run health checks improve data quality, suggest new connections, and support ongoing knowledge base refinement.
